# Descrição original da tarefa no Jira:
Foi criado o novo repositório rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram, para armazenar o código do programa de pontos/hub de recompensas separadamente do código da área do cartão
nesta tarefa devemos migrar todo o código já desenvolvido para o novo repositório e já garantir a nova arquitetura, zustand.

Estou passando a atividade para o @Antonio, que vai finalizar a migração aplicando nova arquitetura zustand no que falta, que é o pointsReducer(onde armazenamos os dados de saldo e products).

--- 

Tarefa para o claude code:
1) Pesquise na internet sobre "Anthropic Harness", depois configure o harness neste projeto se ainda não estiver configurado e seguir com a tarefa sem pausas ou perguntas até a conclusão da migração. A config e arquivos do harness (progress.txt, features.json) devem ficar na pasta `./.claude`

2) O objetivo é seja concluida a migração do código na  
pasta /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place referente ao programa de pontos para este projeto

O conteúdo do programa de pontos está na pasta newArch, mas aqui ele deve ser inserido direto na raiz do src

Também precisamos
- [ ] Os reducers/ducks relacionados a "Points" que importamos no codigo relacionado a recompensas precisa ser migrado para a nova arquitetura zustand, aqui no novo repositorio.
- [ ] Remover coisas que não usamos (rotas, props, ducks)
- [ ] Migrar a WebView Antiga para a nova webview (copiar de /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/atomic/templates/WebViewScreen).
- 
Observações:
- Este projeto é um package micro frontend parte do superapp
  - o superapp fica em `~/dev/appSemPararv3`
- As tipagens typescript e o linter não funcionam corretamente nem no superapp, nem nos microfrontends, mas não precisamos nos preocupar com isso agora
- Estamos movendo o código aos poucos, apenas nas features novas, para a nova arquitetura descrita em `.harness/docs/STYLE_GUIDE.md`
- Localmente usamos este package usando `yarn ync:yalc`, que sincroniza este package com o superApp

# Importante
- Ainda não tente executar o projeto no emulador, apenas verifique os erros com o script typecheck, porém se for erro de tipo apenas e que já existia no repositorio antigo, apenas salve nos arquivos do Harness como observação.
- Nunca faça code push no git, apenas commit cada progresso.

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    12→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    13→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    15→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    16→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    17→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    18→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    19→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    20→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    21→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    22→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Introduction
    27→
    28→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    29→
    30→Key decisions:
    31→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    32→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    33→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    34→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    39→
    40→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    41→
    42→```
    43→screens/Home/
    44→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    45→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    46→└── Models/
    47→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    48→```
    49→
    50→**Models/index.d.ts**
    51→```ts
    52→export interface HomeParams {
    53→  welcome?: string;
    54→}
    55→export interface HomeState {
    56→  greeting: string;
    57→  isLoading: boolean;
    58→}
    59→export interface HomeHandlers {
    60→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    61→}
    62→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    63→```
    64→
    65→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    66→```ts
    67→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from "react";
    68→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from "./Models";
    69→import { useUserStore } from "@/services/user/store"; // Zustand slice example
    70→
    71→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    72→  const name = useUserStore((s) => s.name);
    73→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    74→
    75→  useEffect(() => {
    76→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    77→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    78→  }, []);
    79→
    80→  const greeting = useMemo(
    81→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || "guest"}!`,
    82→    [params?.welcome, name]
    83→  );
    84→
    85→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    86→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    87→  }, []);
    88→
    89→  return { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA };
    90→}
    91→```
    92→
    93→**view.tsx (render)**
    94→```tsx
    95→import React from "react";
    96→import { View } from "react-native";
    97→import { Text, Button } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
    98→import { useHomeScreen } from "./index";
    99→import type { HomeParams } from "./Models";
   100→
   101→type Props = HomeParams;
   102→
   103→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   104→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   105→
   106→  if (isLoading) {
   107→    return (
   108→      <View>
   109→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   110→      </View>
   111→    );
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  return (
   115→    <View>
   116→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   117→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   118→    </View>
   119→  );
   120→}
   121→```
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   126→
   127→```
   128→atomic/atoms/Button/
   129→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   130→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   131→└── Models/
   132→    └── index.d.ts
   133→```
   134→
   135→**Models/index.d.ts**
   136→```ts
   137→export interface ButtonProps {
   138→  text: string;
   139→  disabled?: boolean;
   140→  onPress?: () => void;
   141→}
   142→export interface ButtonState {
   143→  pressed: boolean;
   144→}
   145→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   146→  handlePress: () => void;
   147→}
   148→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   149→```
   150→
   151→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   152→```ts
   153→import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
   154→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from "./Models";
   155→
   156→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   157→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   158→
   159→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   160→    setPressed(true);
   161→    props.onPress?.();
   162→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   163→  }, [props.onPress]);
   164→
   165→  return { pressed, handlePress };
   166→}
   167→```
   168→
   169→**view.tsx (render)**
   170→```tsx
   171→import React from "react";
   172→import { Pressable, View } from "react-native";
   173→import { Text } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
   174→import { useButton } from "./index";
   175→import type { ButtonProps } from "./Models";
   176→
   177→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   178→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   179→
   180→  return (
   181→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   182→      <View>
   183→        <Text>{props.text}{pressed ? " •" : ""}</Text>
   184→      </View>
   185→    </Pressable>
   186→  );
   187→}
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Project Organization
   193→
   194→```
   195→src/
   196→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   197→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   198→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   199→│   ├── atoms/
   200→│   ├── molecules/
   201→│   └── organisms/
   202→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   203→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   204→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   205→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   206→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   207→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   208→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   209→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   210→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   211→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   212→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   213→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   214→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   215→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   216→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   217→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   218→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   219→```
   220→
   221→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Services Architecture
   226→
   227→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   228→
   229→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   230→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   231→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   232→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   233→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   234→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   235→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   236→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   237→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   238→
   239→Principles:
   240→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   241→- No circular dependencies.
   242→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   247→
   248→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   249→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   250→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   251→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   252→
   253→**Slice example**
   254→```ts
   255→import type { StateCreator } from "zustand";
   256→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from "../models/store";
   257→
   258→export type AuthSlice = {
   259→    auth: AuthState;
   260→    authActions: AuthActions
   261→};
   262→
   263→const initialAuth = {
   264→    user: null,
   265→    loading: false,
   266→    error: undefined,
   267→}
   268→
   269→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<AuthSlice, [], [], AuthSlice> = (set) => ({
   270→    auth: initialAuth,
   271→    authActions: {
   272→        setLoading: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, isLoading: v } })),
   273→        setUser: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, user: v } })),
   274→        setError: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, error: v } })),
   275→        reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   276→    }
   277→});
   278→```
   279→
   280→**Root Store**
   281→
   282→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   283→
   284→```ts
   285→import { create } from "zustand";
   286→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";
   287→import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
   288→
   289→import { createUserSlice, type UserSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user";
   290→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-yourmf";
   291→
   292→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   293→
   294→/** Add your slice Type */
   295→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   296→
   297→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   298→  devtools(
   299→    persist(
   300→      immer((...a) => ({
   301→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   302→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   303→      })),
   304→      {
   305→        name: "root-storage",
   306→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   307→        partialize: (s) => ({
   308→          user: s.user,
   309→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   310→        }),
   311→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({ ...current, ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>) }),
   312→      }
   313→    ),
   314→    { name: "RootStore" }
   315→  )
   316→);
   317→```
   318→
   319→- For Use on Class.
   320→
   321→```ts
   322→class Auth {
   323→  private readonly setAuth;
   324→
   325→  constructor () {
   326→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  function doSomething(data) {
   330→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   331→  }
   332→}
   333→```
   334→
   335→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   336→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   337→
   338→```ts
   339→const loading = useRootStore((s) => s.user.isLoading);
   340→const token = useRootStore((s) => s.auth.token);
   341→```
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## Routes Naming
   346→
   347→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   348→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   349→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   350→
   351→---
   352→
   353→## Folder Naming Rules
   354→
   355→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                    |
   356→|-------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|
   357→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails`  |
   358→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`    |
   359→
   360→---
   361→
   362→## Code Standards
   363→
   364→**Language**: English only.  
   365→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   366→
   367→**Explanatory variables**
   368→```js
   369→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   370→const getNumberValidation = (message) => (value) => !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   371→const isNumber = getNumberValidation("error message");
   372→isNumber(1);
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Semantic constants**
   376→```js
   377→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   378→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   379→```
   380→
   381→**Library workarounds with link**
   382→```js
   383→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   384→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   385→event.target.value = "";
   386→```
   387→
   388→**JSDoc**
   389→```js
   390→/**
   391→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   392→ * @param {*} eventName
   393→ * @param {*} maxLength
   394→ */
   395→```
   396→
   397→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   398→```ts
   399→useEffect(() => {
   400→  (async () => {
   401→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   402→    setSum(42);
   403→  })();
   404→}, [
   405→  setSum,
   406→]);
   407→```
   408→
   409→**Extract conditions**
   410→```ts
   411→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   412→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   413→```
   414→
   415→**Optional chaining**
   416→```ts
   417→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   418→```
   419→
   420→**JSX indentation**
   421→```tsx
   422→{
   423→  !isEnd && (
   424→    <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />
   425→  )
   426→}
   427→```
   428→
   429→**Early returns**
   430→```ts
   431→if (!data) return null;
   432→return <View />;
   433→```
   434→
   435→**Nullish coalescing**
   436→```ts
   437→return data ?? 0;
   438→```
   439→---
   440→
   441→## Styles Patterns
   442→
   443→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   444→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→## Cache Pattern
   449→
   450→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   451→
   452→```ts
   453→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   454→
   455→export interface CachedData<T> {
   456→  timestamp: number;
   457→  data: T;
   458→}
   459→
   460→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   461→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   462→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   463→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   464→}
   465→
   466→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   467→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   468→
   469→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   470→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   471→    if (!json) return null;
   472→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   473→  }
   474→
   475→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   476→    const payload: CachedData<T> = { timestamp: Date.now(), data };
   477→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   478→  }
   479→
   480→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   481→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   482→  }
   483→}
   484→```
   485→
   486→**Guidelines**
   487→- One storage class per module/feature.
   488→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   489→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   490→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   491→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   492→
   493→---
   494→
   495→## Checklist
   496→
   497→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   498→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   499→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   500→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   501→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   502→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   503→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   504→

## Comprehensive Project Structure Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram` project, here's what I found:

### 1. Current Folder Structure in `src/`

The project is a React Native microfrontend (MF) using the hook component pattern with Zustand for state management:

```
src/
├── @types/                    # TypeScript definitions
├── assets/                    # Images, fonts, icons
├── atomic/                    # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
│   ├── atoms/
│   ├── organisms/
│   └── templates/             # BasicScreen, Screen, WrapperKeyboardAvoiding
├── components/
├── constants/
├── hooks/                     # Cross-cutting React hooks
├── modules/                   # Microfrontends shared modules
├── routes/
├── screens/                   # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
│   ├── Example/
│   ├── Extract/
│   ├── InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/
│   ├── InvoiceCashbackPoints/
│   ├── InvoiceCashbackSummary/
│   ├── LearnMore/
│   ├── Marketplace/
│   ├── MyOrders/
│   ├── Points/                # Already using Hook pattern + Redux (needs migration)
│   ├── PointsDetail/
│   └── Splash/
├── services/                  # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
│   ├── api/                   # HTTP layer (Axios)
│   ├── hooks/
│   ├── models/                # Domain types & contracts
│   ├── repository/            # Data abstraction & mapping
│   ├── services/              # Business orchestration
│   ├── store/                 # **Zustand slices (NEW ARCHITECTURE)**
│   └── utils/
├── redux/                     # **Old Redux/Redux-Saga (being migrated away)**
│   ├── actions/
│   ├── ducks/                 # Redux action creators & reducers
│   ├── reducers/
│   └── sagas/
├── settings/
├── styles/
└── utils/
```

### 2. Migration Status (What's Been Done)

**Already Migrated to Zustand:**
- `/src/services/store/extract.store.ts` - Points extract data (transactions, balance, filters)
- `/src/services/store/parameter.store.ts` - Parameter Points Program data
- `/src/services/store/airlineMiles.store.ts` - Airline Miles data
- `/src/services/store/invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts` - Invoice Cashback permission

**Still Using Redux:**
- `/src/redux/ducks/points.ts` - **Main Points reducer with complex state** (balance, products, extract data, filters, parameterization)
- Various screen components still importing from `redux/ducks/points`
- Screen components still using `connect()` from react-redux with `mapStateToProps`

**Files Importing Redux Points Ducks:**
- `/src/screens/Points/index.tsx` - Connect using redux
- `/src/screens/PointsDetail/index.tsx` - Using PointsActions
- `/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx` - Using PointsActions
- `/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx` - Using PointsActions
- `/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx` - Using PointsActions
- `/src/atomic/organisms/FiltersBottomSheet/index.tsx` - Using FilterOption type
- `/src/hooks/custom/useCardBuilderWhereUseMyPoints/index.ts` - Using PointsProduct type

### 3. .claude Folder & Configuration

**Exists:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.claude/`

**Contents:**
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project-specific instructions (tasks checklist)
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md` - **Architecture Guide** (13,340 bytes - comprehensive!)

**Status:** `.harness` directory does NOT exist. Only `.claude` exists with STYLE_GUIDE.md.

### 4. Existing Zustand Stores

All properly implemented following the STYLE_GUIDE pattern:

**Extract Store** (`/src/services/store/extract.store.ts`):
- State: `pointsExtract`, `pointsBalance`, `pointsExtractFilters`
- Actions: `setExtractStatus`, `setExtractData`, `appendExtractData`, `setLoadingMore`, `setBalanceStatus`, `setBalance`, `setTransactionsFilters`, `clearTransactionsFilters`, `reset`
- Uses `StateCreator` pattern with proper TypeScript typing

**Parameter Store** (`/src/services/store/parameter.store.ts`):
- State: `parameterPointsProgram`
- Actions: `setStatus`, `setData`, `setSelectedCategory`, `reset`

**Root Store** (`/src/store/index.ts`):
- Uses Zustand `create()` with middleware: `devtools`, `persist`, `immer`
- Persists: `parameterPointsProgram` only
- Uses custom storage: `~/redux/store/storage`
- Combines all slices: `PointsExtractSlice`, `ParameterPointsProgramSlice`, `AirlineMilesSlice`, `InvoiceCashbackPermissionSlice`

### 5. Source Material from card-place Project

**Points Redux Duck** (`/src/redux/ducks/points.ts` in card-place):
- Contains complex state with 18+ properties
- Types: REQUEST/SUCCESS/FAILED for Balance, Products, Extract, Statement, Rescue, Parameterization
- Models: `PointsProduct`, `PointsTransaction`, `ExpiringPoints`, `FilterOption`, `PointsExtractData`, `ProcessedExtractData`, `AppliedFilters`
- Complex processing logic (e.g., JSON parsing for `pointsRescueSentence`)

**newArch Store** (`/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/` in card-place):
- Already refactored to SOLID layout:
  - `api/` - Parameter, AirlineMiles, Permission APIs
  - `models/` - Type definitions
  - `repository/` - Data mapping
  - `services/` - Business logic
  - `store/` - Zustand slices
  - `hooks/` - Custom hooks
  - `utils/` - Helpers

**WebViewScreen** (`/src/atomic/templates/WebViewScreen/` in card-place):
- Simple component wrapper (view.tsx + index.ts + Models/index.ts)
- Does NOT exist in reward program project yet

### 6. STYLE_GUIDE.md Key Points

**Architecture Principles:**
- **Exclusive Zustand** for global/shared state
- **Hook Component Pattern**: logic hook (`index.ts`) + view component (`view.tsx`)
- **SOLID Services** layout with API, Repository, Services, Store layers
- **No Redux** - Redux-Saga completely removed in new architecture
- Types in `/Models` directories
- No StyleSheet or styled-components (use design-system)
- Cache pattern with `GenericStorage<T>`

**Folder Naming Convention:**
- `atomic`, `screens`, `routes` → PascalCase
- `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, `store` → camelCase

**Integration:**
- UI never calls API directly
- All side effects in logic hooks calling services
- Services orchestrate business logic
- Store selectors used only via Zustand hooks

### 7. Critical Findings

**Issues to Address:**
1. **Points reducer not migrated** - Still using Redux (major blocker)
2. **Multiple imports from `redux/ducks/points`** across 11 files
3. **Redux connection pattern** still used (e.g., `connect()` in Points screen)
4. **No `.harness` configuration** - Only `.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md` exists
5. **WebViewScreen missing** - Needs to be copied from card-place
6. **Type duplication** - Some types exist in Redux ducks and models (redundancy)

### 8. File Paths Summary

**Current Project Root:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram`

**Key Configuration Files:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.claude/CLAUDE.md` - Task instructions
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md` - Architecture guide
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/store/index.ts` - Root Zustand store
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/store/slices.ts` - Store slices composition

**Source for Migration:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/redux/ducks/points.ts` - Points reducer (needs migration)
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/` - Reference implementation
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/atomic/templates/WebViewScreen/` - WebViewScreen template

**Redux Imports to Replace:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/redux/ducks/points.ts` - Being phased out
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/redux/store/` - Old Redux setup

This is a well-structured microfrontend in transition from Redux to Zustand, following the Sem Parar architecture guide. The primary work remaining is the complete migration of the Points reducer to Zustand and refactoring dependent screens to use the Hook Component pattern with store selectors instead of Redux connect.
